steps.title: Identify the cause-effect relationship
steps.content: The passage shows: he joined the Army, then served in the cavalry, then earned a place in history. The blank must express how the third action flows from the second.

steps.title: Match the causal link
steps.content:
The logical relationship is means-to-result: by serving in the cavalry, Johnson earned his place in history. The blank needs a transition signaling that the action in the preceding clause is how the result was achieved.
  • Choice A — usually,: signals habitual frequency, so fails — this is a specific individual's story, not a general pattern. ✗
  • Choice B — for instance,: signals a specific example of a general claim, so fails — no general claim precedes this that cavalry service would illustrate. ✗
  • Choice C — in any case,: signals a concession or change of subject, so fails — the sentence is building forward, not dismissing what came before. ✗
  • Choice D — in doing so,: signals that through the preceding action he achieved the result, so fits — his cavalry service is how he earned his place in history. ✓
D is correct.
